text.title: prototyping Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for prototyping as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Prototype built to too high a fidelity before any user testing has been conducted.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: High-fidelity prototypes take weeks to build. If the first user test reveals a fundamental navigation problem, the investment is discarded. Low-fidelity is appropriate until the structure is validated.

failure-mode.description: Prototype with no defined learning objective — testing "the whole product."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unfocused prototypes produce unfocused findings. Testers cannot distinguish signal from noise; the synthesis session produces a list of problems with no prioritization basis.

failure-mode.description: Findings documented as raw session notes, not discrete prioritized issues.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Engineering teams cannot act on raw qualitative notes. Findings must be synthesized into discrete, severity-rated issues before sharing — otherwise they are ignored in favor of concrete backlog items.

failure-mode.description: Prototype overwrites previous version — no version history maintained.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Design decisions frequently regress when new constraints emerge. Without previous versions accessible, teams rebuild earlier explorations from memory.

failure-mode.description: High-fidelity Figma prototype treated as a complete specification for engineers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Figma hotspots communicate navigation but not state transitions, animation timing, conditional behavior, or edge cases. An interaction spec document is always required alongside the prototype.

failure-mode.description: LLMs generating prototype code do not know the design system; inject component library docs or a token file into context first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent-generated code uses arbitrary component APIs; providing design system context ensures consistency.

failure-mode.description: Agents over-scope: constrain to a single named flow in the prompt or the output will be unmanageable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without explicit scope constraint, agents attempt to build the entire product, producing unmaintainable code.

failure-mode.description: Figma API returns node IDs, not screen names — pre-map IDs to names before passing to an agent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents cannot parse node IDs meaningfully; pre-mapping enables agent to reference screens by human-readable names.

failure-mode.description: Generated React prototypes often use useState in ways that break multi-step flows; agent should be told to use a simple state machine or step counter.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without explicit state management guidance, agents generate fragile state handling.

failure-mode.description: Prototype quality review requires a human designer — do not let an agent declare the prototype "ready for testing".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents cannot assess design quality; human review is the only valid quality gate.

failure-mode.description: When agents write session scripts (facilitator guides), they omit think-aloud instructions; always append the standard preamble manually.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents produce generic scripts without the human-centric facilitation guidance needed for valid testing.
